ActionItems: Hold membership at $85/mo; revisit if lease renewal exceeds $1,500.
Grow active roster from 45 toward 50 to rebuild equipment reserve to $2,600.
Add one Saturday kids block (Tomás) to lift drop-in revenue.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Roots Martial Arts Academy — 1615 Broadway, Sacramento. Break-even sensitivity on the studio lease ahead of the Dec 1, 2026 lease/insurance renewal.

CurrentState.Note: 45 active members at $85/mo. Net ~$1,000–$1,200/mo retained for reserves and equipment fund.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: At $85 dues and ~$2,795 fixed monthly cost, break-even is ~33 members. Current 45 members leaves comfortable margin; a rent bump to $850 above base is absorbable, $1,500+ is not without a dues increase.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: If a smaller space at $750 became available, break-even drops to ~26 members; net rises ~$650/mo.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Relocate to $750/mo space

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Lease renewal at $850 above current base would push fixed cost to ~$2,245 and break-even to ~30 members; still sustainable at 45.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Lease renewal to $850 base increase

Scenario_C_RenewalTo1650.BreakEvenCalculation: Full monthly obligation under the renewal = Lease $1,650 + Insurance $180 + Utilities $120 + Equipment fund $200 + InstructorPay $480 + Cleaning $220 + Supplies $160 + Marketing $120 + OwnerDraw $1,000 + ReserveContribution $290 = $4,420. At $85 dues, $4,420 ÷ $85 = 52 members to fully cover the obligation.
Scenario_C_RenewalTo1650.Impact: Landlord floated the Dec 1 renewal at $1,650 (up $250 from the current $1,400). Rebuilt from all current components the full monthly obligation the dues must cover is $4,420, not the stale March figure. A reader who tallies only the recurring operating lines and skips the $200 equipment fund plus the $55 utilities true-up lands at $4,165 → 49 members, which understates the true break-even by three students.
Scenario_C_RenewalTo1650.Label: Proposed Dec 1 renewal, lease to $1,650
